text.title: consistency-standards Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for consistency-standards as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Prematurely locking in a consistency standard during early exploration before validating patterns with users.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Locking in too early means building the design system around an unvalidated pattern, which forces redesign or inconsistency when user research contradicts the pattern.

failure-mode.description: Running consistency audits as a one-time big-bang exercise and expecting the result to hold without ongoing governance.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Inconsistency drifts immediately after the audit if there is no design system owner and no process to catch new violations. Ongoing audits (per component or feature) catch drift earlier and cost less to fix.
